name: Neutral Jump Heavy Kick
notes: Longest horizontal range of Viper's air normals; in the corner or against wide characters (Blanka/E.Honda/Zangief), can combo Punish Counter 5HK into High Jump cancel nj.HK for powerful combo extension; lower body projectile invuln 10-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

name: Parry Drive Rush
notes: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.374 (min/throw), 1.756 (min/block), 3.355 (max/final DR frame)

name: Drive Rush Cancel
notes: See Drive Rush; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

name: High Jump
notes: Costs 1 bar of Drive gauge (except during SA1 install); jumps slightly higher and over 50% farther than a standard jump; can be input in neutral or canceled on hit/block from all normals (except 2HK/6MP/6HK) and from Seismic Hammer; can be input as a forward or neutral jump (cannot High Jump backwards, but attempting this as a cancel results in neutral jump); cancelable into grounded special moves and Supers during prejump frames 2-6, effectively allowing any HJ-cancelable attack to be special/super cancelable; C. Viper is in a Counter-hit state for the entire duration and in a Forced Knockdown state when airborne; Drive Gauge recovery acts similar to OD specials (can start regaining meter quickly, effectively refunding part of its cost by hitting the opponent afterward; grounded High Jump Cancel into grounded Burning Kick cannot be buffered on the first jump frame (results in a strict 2f window to properly input a combo sequence like juggled 5HK > HJC OD Burning Kick); can be used in safe jump setups with a KD Advantage of +45; the Up direction must be input no later than the 8th frame after initiating the Down direction (holding down before inputting a jump does not result in a High Jump)

name: Thunder Dash Feint
notes: Feint animation lasts 12f but occurs at a different timing from each meterless strength of Thunder Dash (LP 12+12 / MP 8+12 / HP 6+12); can be input more easily by holding any K while inputting 214P; 214HP version gives better combo potential from 2HP or from High Jump canceled 5HP/5HK; Counter-hit state for entire feint animation

name: Tracer Combination
notes: Costs 1 extra bar of Drive gauge (except during SA1 install); maintains the Projectile Invuln and crouch-sized hurtbox of 214LP on frames 1-17; best version for confirming into SA3 (combos naturally); forms a true blockstring from 214LP; gives solid oki with Drive Rush or a Forward Dash + manually timed walk

name: Tracer Combination
notes: Costs 1 extra bar of Drive gauge (except during SA1 install); cancel into SA3 does not combo on grounded opponents; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle OTG bounce state (KD +30 at lowest height, allows SA3 cancel to juggle); 2f gap on block can set up a risky frame trap (no gap during Burnout or if the final 2 active frames of 214MP connect); grounded hit gives the worst oki of all Thunder Dash follow-ups (-3 after Dash)

name: Tracer Combination
notes: Costs 1 extra bar of Drive gauge (except during SA1 install); can only be canceled into on hit; can whiff on high juggled opponents; gives the best oki out of all Thunder Dash follow-ups; useful way to extend C. Viper's anti-air damage

name: Knuckled Pursuit
notes: Costs 1 extra bar of Drive gauge (except during SA1 install); true blockstring and natural combo from Burning Kick; higher KD advantage if Burning Kick lands as a Punish Counter or hits airborne opponent; KD +9 when follow-up hits a grounded opponent (e.g. after Burning Kick is absorbed by 1-hit armor like Luke 236KK or Zangief Charged 5HP)

name: Double Burn
notes: Costs 1 extra bar of Drive gauge (except during SA1 install); leaves a 3f gap on block from Burning Kick that can beat non-invincible attacks (but loses to Drive Impact and anti-air invuln moves); true blockstring vs. opponents in Burnout and +6 oB making it a great pressure tool; KD +40 on grounded opponents (e.g. frame trap after Burning Kick) and +34 on airborne opponents (e.g. combo after PC Burning Kick or in juggle routes, up to KD +40 in very high juggles); does not combo naturally from Burning Kick on grounded opponents (requires Punish Counter or airborne connect); whiffing this attack on grounded hit results in a +13 knockdown with oki only possible in the corner

name: Seismic Hammer Feint
notes: Useful for baiting neutral responses like jumps or anti-projectile Supers; much slower than HP Thunder Dash feint (not useful for combos/pressure when canceled from normals); can only be performed from meterless Seismic Hammer after 11 frames of startup (27f total animation); can be performed by holding K for easier execution; Counter-hit state for entire feint animation

name: Focus Force (Lv.2)
notes: Partially charged Lv.2 version when button is held at least 20f (up to 58f); Punish Counter causes a limited juggle crumple state (+8 before opponent falls to the ground); 1-hit armor frames cover the full body, ending immediately if button is released after frame 13; attack hitbox starts up 10f after button release (no armor on these frames); absorbing a strike results in an automatic counter-attack with 10f startup; C. Viper incurs only 7f armor hitstop from absorbing a strike; armor can absorb projectiles but does not perform counter-attack (C. Viper incurs 10f projectile hitstop during armor); cancelable into Forward Dash from frame 12 until button is released (not possible after absorbing a strike); C. Viper is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration (including Forward Dash cancel); only the first hit gains the bonus damage from a Counter-hit/Punish Counter; -17 on Punish Counter if only the first hit of counter-attack reaches opponent (e.g. absorbing a ranged Dhalsim poke); Focus + Dash can be used to absorb projectiles when Drive gauge is too low for Parry

name: Focus Force (Lv.2)
notes: Partially charged Lv.2 version when button is held at least 20f (up to 56f); Punish Counter causes a limited juggle crumple state (+8 before opponent falls to the ground); 1-hit armor frames cover the full body, ending immediately if button is released after frame 10; attack hitbox starts up 10f after button release (no armor on these frames); absorbing a strike results in an automatic counter-attack with 10f startup; C. Viper incurs only 7f armor hitstop from absorbing a strike; armor can absorb projectiles but does not perform counter-attack (C. Viper incurs 10f projectile hitstop during armor); cancelable into Forward Dash from frame 9 until button is released (not possible after absorbing a strike); C. Viper is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration (including Forward Dash cancel); only the first hit gains the bonus damage from a Counter-hit/Punish Counter; -17 on Punish Counter if only the first hit of counter-attack reaches opponent (e.g. absorbing a ranged Dhalsim poke)

name: Focus Force (Lv.3)
notes: Fully charged Lv.3 version when button is held at least 59f; Punish Counter causes a limited juggle crumple state (+29 before opponent falls to the ground); 1-hit armor frames cover the full body; attack hitbox starts up 10f after reaching full charge (no armor on these frames); absorbing a strike while charging results in an automatic counter-attack with 10f startup; C. Viper incurs only 7f armor hitstop from absorbing a strike; armor can absorb projectiles but does not perform counter-attack (C. Viper incurs 10f projectile hitstop during armor); cancelable into Forward Dash from frame 12-60 (not possible after absorbing a strike); C. Viper is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration (including Forward Dash cancel); only the first hit gains the bonus damage from a Counter-hit/Punish Counter; -17 on Punish Counter if only the first hit of counter-attack reaches opponent (e.g. absorbing a ranged Dhalsim poke)

name: Focus Force (Lv.3)
notes: Fully charged Lv.3 version when button is held at least 57f; Punish Counter causes a limited juggle crumple state (+29 before opponent falls to the ground); extremely powerful combo starter for Stun or punishing very unsafe Supers; 1-hit armor frames cover the full body; attack hitbox starts up 10f after reaching full charge (no armor on these frames); absorbing a strike while charging results in an automatic counter-attack with 10f startup; C. Viper incurs only 7f armor hitstop from absorbing a strike; armor can absorb projectiles but does not perform counter-attack (C. Viper incurs 10f projectile hitstop during armor); cancelable into Forward Dash from frame 9-58 (not possible after absorbing a strike); C. Viper is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration (including Forward Dash cancel); only the first hit gains the bonus damage from a Counter-hit/Punish Counter; -17 on Punish Counter if only the first hit of counter-attack reaches opponent (e.g. absorbing a ranged Dhalsim poke)

name: Neutral Taunt
notes: "Ugh, could you just like, give up now? I have other work to do."

name: Forward Taunt
notes: "I hate to admit, you're not half bad! Even messed up my makeup. Hmm, alright, all done up."

name: Back Taunt
notes: Begins with "You're a stubborn one —" / "You're becoming a pain in —" / "I have a few new tricks up —" / "Decouple power limiters —" / "This is going on far too —" / "I think it's about time I —"; after being interrupted, plays out one of 6 potential phone conversations.
